The total number of pegs is \(5 + 4 + 3 + 2 + 1 = 15\), which matches the number of positions in the triangular board. To ensure no row or column contains two or more pegs of the same color, we need to place each color in a different row and column. This is equivalent to finding the number of ways to permute 5 colors in 5 rows, then 4 colors in the remaining 4 rows, and so on. This is calculated as \(5! \times 4! \times 3! \times 2! \times 1!\). Calculating each factorial, we get \(5! = 120\), \(4! = 24\), \(3! = 6\), \(2! = 2\), and \(1! = 1\). Multiplying these together gives \(120 \times 24 \times 6 \times 2 \times 1 = 34560\). </think> <answer> 34560 \</answer>
